items.title: For the final few months of the 2025-26 season, a blue and orange Ariel Hukporti jersey was hanging in the Sixers' locker room.
items.author: Adam Aaronson
items.body_excerpt:
For the final few months of the 2025-26 season, a blue and orange Ariel Hukporti jersey was hanging in the Sixers' locker room.

Now, they can change the colors, as the Sixers appear set to bank on a trio of young players to handle backing up Joel Embiid:

www.phillyvoice.com/report-sixer...

media.title: Report: Sixers signing Ariel Hukporti to one-year, $3.4 million contract
media.description: Ariel Hukporti has 79 NBA games under his belt. The Sixers believe the soon-to-be third-year big can improve their backup center situation.

items.title: After adding Dean Wade and Ariel Hukporti, I've got the Philadelphia 76ers about $13.2M under their first-apron hard cap (triggered by using Non-Taxpayer MLE for Wade/Hukporti).
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
After adding Dean Wade and Ariel Hukporti, I've got the Philadelphia 76ers about $13.2M under their first-apron hard cap (triggered by using Non-Taxpayer MLE for Wade/Hukporti).

Two open standard roster spots for the Sixers. Kelly Oubre Jr still a free agent.

items.title: Ariel Hukporti got a little more than the veteran minimum for a player with 2 years of service.
items.author: Austin Krell
items.body_excerpt:
Ariel Hukporti got a little more than the veteran minimum for a player with 2 years of service.

The only way for Sixers to pay a player more than the minimum is to use a piece of the remaining MLE or the bi-annual exception.

items.body_excerpt:
Ariel Hukporti is getting some of what's left of the Non-Taxpayer MLE (most went to Dean Wade) from the Philadelphia 76ers.

May take a return for Andre Drummond to the Sixers out of the mix.

items.title: Free agent center Ariel Hukporti has agreed to a one-year, $3.4 million deal with the Philadelphia 76ers, sources tell ESPN. Hukporti departs the Knicks after playing a reserve role on their title team, and gives the 76ers frontcourt depth and a high-motor, athletic big man.
items.author: Shams Charania Tweets
items.body_excerpt: Free agent center Ariel Hukporti has agreed to a one-year, $3.4 million deal with the Philadelphia 76ers, sources tell ESPN. Hukporti departs the Knicks after playing a reserve role on their title team, and gives the 76ers frontcourt depth and a high-motor, athletic big man.
